iteration_log.summary: Iter1 revisions to ticket markdown only (context JSON otherwise unchanged): OBJ-1 narrowed AC3 to agent_id-only (applied in prior partial dispatch before rate limit). OBJ-2 removed read_grant referrers from AC2 + AC7 so both require literal exact JSON body matches. OBJ-3 rewrote subagent-handling prose at line 51 and Risk 4 to state subagents are unconditionally blocked by PATCH-2 (no /do or /allow path for subagents in this cycle), matching the patch's `if [ "$_BEW_IS_SUB" != "1" ]` gating. OBJ-4 resolved BEFORE/BETWEEN contradiction at lines 230+237 by selecting the BETWEEN interpretation (bypass inserted AFTER is_exempt, BEFORE line_count enforcement; code blocks byte-identical). OBJ-5 reconciled AC9 enumeration count: line 203 opener now reads '9 files (8 already-honoring hooks plus stop-cleanup-allowlist.sh housekeeping)' matching the 9-item list; line 205 simplified to drop the inconsistent 8+2 housekeeping arithmetic.

requirement.original: /dev 全修 — fix every bug found by the prior Codex audit relating to the /do and /allow slash-command bypass coverage across the pretool hooks under /root/.claude/hooks/. The user's verbatim grievance was that /do failed to unlock Read on large files even though /do claims to grant the main agent subagent-class permissions; they had to delegate to a subagent to get the work done. They want the bypass contract to actually hold.
requirement.clarified: Make /do and /allow honor their documented contract in the two PreToolUse hooks where the contract is currently broken: (1) pretool-read-size-guard.py — add /do consent flag + /allow Read grant bypass for main-agent Read calls, and restore LINE_LIMIT=600 to reconcile with docstring and CLAUDE.md; (2) pretool-block-enterworktree.sh — add /do consent flag + /allow EnterWorktree grant bypass for main-agent EnterWorktree calls. Do not modify any other hook.
requirement.what: Insert /do consent flag check and /allow grant check into pretool-read-size-guard.py (Python) and pretool-block-enterworktree.sh (Bash). Restore LINE_LIMIT=600.
requirement.why: The /do command writes /tmp/claude-orchestrator-consent-<sid>.flag and is documented (CLAUDE.md:49) as granting the main agent subagent-class permissions for all tools except EnterPlanMode/ExitPlanMode. Two PreToolUse hooks do not honor this flag, breaking the user-visible contract. The user explicitly experienced the failure on Read of a large file.

included: Add /do bypass branch to pretool-read-size-guard.py for main-agent Read
Add /allow Read grant bypass branch to pretool-read-size-guard.py for main-agent Read
Restore LINE_LIMIT=600 in pretool-read-size-guard.py (matches docstring + CLAUDE.md)
Convert pretool-block-enterworktree.sh from CLAUDE_TOOL_NAME env to stdin JSON reader
Add /do bypass branch to pretool-block-enterworktree.sh for main-agent EnterWorktree
Add /allow EnterWorktree grant bypass branch to pretool-block-enterworktree.sh
excluded: Modifying pretool-workflow-gate.py (different contract; out-of-scope per FINDING-2)
Modifying any of the 8 already-honoring hooks listed by the Codex audit
Adding new restrictions, defensive hardening, or refactors
Broadening or narrowing the /allow matching semantics in lib/allowlist.py
Touching subagent agent_id exemption semantics (existing behavior preserved)

success_criteria: Main-agent Read of a 601-line file passes when /do flag set
Main-agent Read of a 601-line file passes when /allow Read granted
Subagent Reads still bypass unconditionally (no regression)
Plain main-agent Read of >600-line file without /do/allow/bounded-chunk still blocks
Main-agent EnterWorktree passes when /do flag set
Main-agent EnterWorktree passes when /allow EnterWorktree granted
Plain main-agent EnterWorktree without /do/allow still blocks with original safety message
constraints: Use canonical has_consent idiom from pretool-orchestrator-gate.py:66-71 (existence + content == 'true', not bare flag.exists())
Use lib.allowlist.read_grant() — do NOT delete grant files in PreToolUse hooks (deletion stays in posttool-allowlist-consume.py)
Fail-open on JSON parse errors (matches every other PreToolUse hook)
Subagent agent_id branch must still win unconditionally

out_of_path_observed_but_not_touched: Other all-agent safety hooks (bash-views-guard, claude-config-guard, block-production, etc.)

out_of_scope_observations.observation: Hook blocks any non-TodoWrite/TodoRead tool while a workflow bookmark exists and todo_acknowledged is False or locked. No subagent exemption, no /do bypass, no /allow bypass. Codex audit flagged this as 'contract ambiguity'. Out-of-scope because: (a) the hook only fires when a workflow bookmark exists (i.e., during /dev or /close); (b) CLAUDE.md does not document /do as bypassing workflow checklists — it documents /do as bypassing the orchestrator-gate's tool-class tiering; (c) the hook has no subagent exemption, so there is no subagent-parity gap to repair — main agent and subagents are treated identically by this hook by design. If the user later wants /do to bypass workflow checklists, that requires a separate ticket and a contract clarification.

read_grant() PreToolUse matching uses exact_or_substr semantics (pattern == tool_name OR pattern in tool_name), while consume_grant_for_posttool branch 3 (non-Bash literal) uses exact-only (pattern == tool_name). Codex observation #9 flagged the asymmetry as an adjacent issue: a user issuing /allow Re would substring-match a 'Read' tool call at PreToolUse but fail to consume at PostToolUse, potentially leading to grant leakage. Not in this ticket's user-need path. All new call sites added by this ticket follow the same exact_or_substr semantics as the 8 existing call sites in the repo — we do not change read_grant.

Codex audit listed 9 hooks (bash-views-guard, block-production-files, block-production, claude-config-guard, overnight-hook-guard, worktree-guard, todo-validate, spec-block-foreground-agent, tool-policy, cp-state-write-guard) as 'not strict /do bugs'. They enforce different invariants (production-file protection, worktree confinement, view/cp-state write protection). They block main agent and subagents alike — no subagent-parity gap exists. CLAUDE.md's '/do unlocks everything except EnterPlanMode/ExitPlanMode' framing is about orchestrator-gate tool-class tiering, not about safety/integrity hooks that intentionally cover everyone. Out-of-scope.

root_cause_analysis.symptom: Main agent invokes /do, then tries to Read a large file, hook still exit-2 blocks. User forced to delegate to a subagent.
root_cause_analysis.root_cause: pretool-read-size-guard.py never reads the /do consent flag at /tmp/claude-orchestrator-consent-<sid>.flag and never calls lib.allowlist.read_grant for /allow Read grants. The hook has a subagent agent_id exemption at lines 21-25 but no main-agent consent/grant carve-out. CLAUDE.md and commands/do.md both document /do as granting main-agent subagent-class permissions for all tools except EnterPlanMode/ExitPlanMode — including Read — but the hook itself never received the wiring. A parallel gap exists in pretool-block-enterworktree.sh for the documented /do EnterWorktree carve-out.
root_cause_analysis.root_cause_commit: Original gap: never added to the hook from inception. Closely-related commits: 150efdb (Apr 16 — raised LINE_LIMIT 200→600 but did not wire /do); 9204c25 (May 7 — raised LINE_LIMIT 600→1000 and added CHUNK_LIMIT carve-out as LOW-9 inside a daily-trade defects batch, still did not wire /do).
root_cause_analysis.why_introduced: When the read-size guard was added, the /do/allow bypass framework existed only in pretool-orchestrator-gate.py. Subsequent tweaks (150efdb, 9204c25) treated symptoms (threshold raise, chunk carve-out) without addressing the underlying bypass-coverage gap. pretool-block-enterworktree.sh predates the /do contract being formalized in CLAUDE.md.
root_cause_analysis.why_problematic: The user-facing contract in CLAUDE.md (and commands/do.md) is that /do grants main-agent subagent-class permissions for all non-permanently-blocked tools. Two hooks silently violate this contract, forcing the user to use the very subagent-delegation workaround that /do is designed to eliminate.
root_cause_analysis.timeline: pretool-block-enterworktree.sh predates the /do contract; pretool-read-size-guard.py first introduced Apr 15 (commit f5241eb checkpoint), tweaked Apr 16 (150efdb) and May 7 (9204c25), bypass gap present from day one.
observations: pretool-read-size-guard.py reads stdin JSON, never opens any /tmp/claude-orchestrator-consent-*.flag file (observed: grep + file Read confirmed no such call)
pretool-read-size-guard.py does not import lib.allowlist (observed: grep on the file confirms import block is json/os/sys only)
Codex independently invoked the hook with /do flag set + /allow Read granted on a 1001-line file and got exit 2 (observed: codex audit log section 'Verified behavior')
Codex invoked the same hook with agent_id set and got exit 0 (observed: codex audit log)
pretool-block-enterworktree.sh reads CLAUDE_TOOL_NAME env var only — no JSON parse, no flag check, no grant check (observed: file Read at hooks/pretool-block-enterworktree.sh:1-24)
CLAUDE.md:49 (observed: Read of /root/.claude/CLAUDE.md) documents /do as unlocking everything except EnterPlanMode/ExitPlanMode
commands/do.md description (observed: Read of file) confirms 'Allow main agent to bypass orchestrator-gate restrictions for this turn (subagent-only operations become directly allowed)'
root_cause_analysis.localization_evidence: Codex audit reproduced the failure by direct hook invocation with /do flag and /allow Read grant pre-set; hook returned exit 2. I independently reproduced the same code path by reading pretool-read-size-guard.py:1-75 and confirming: no consent flag check, no read_grant call, no /allow import. Symptom-to-source path: user Read tool call → PreToolUse hook chain → pretool-read-size-guard.py main() → is_exempt() returns False for main agent → line count > LIMIT → sys.exit(2).

git_bisect_result.bisect_blocked: Not a regression in the usual sense — the bypass gap was present from inception. Git log shows two threshold-only tweaks (150efdb, 9204c25) that did not address the bypass gap.

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: 全修 (fix every bug found by prior Codex audit relating to /do and /allow bypass coverage); '/do 还是出问题' (why does /do still have problems)
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: After patch: with /do consent flag set, main-agent Read on >600-line file exits 0; with /allow Read granted, same path exits 0; subagent Reads unaffected; plain main-agent >600-line Read without consent still blocks; same parity for EnterWorktree per CLAUDE.md carve-out; 9 already-honoring hooks unchanged.

component_chain.role: Block site for main-agent Read on files exceeding LINE_LIMIT. Receives PreToolUse JSON on stdin, exits 0 if exempt or under limit, exits 2 with stderr message otherwise.
Block site for EnterWorktree tool (any actor). Reads CLAUDE_TOOL_NAME env var, exits 0 if not EnterWorktree, exits 2 with stderr safety message otherwise.
Canonical read_grant(tool_name, sid) function — single source of truth for /allow grant matching at PreToolUse. Returns True on match, False otherwise. Does NOT delete grant files.
Canonical has_consent(session_id) helper — single source of truth for /do consent flag check. Returns True only if flag exists and content is exactly 'true'.
Canonical bash-side idiom for /do + /allow bypass: stdin JSON parse, agent_id check, consent flag content == 'true' check, inline python bridge calling lib.allowlist.read_grant.

pre_existing_guards.purpose: Early-exit non-Read tool calls — the hook is registered globally but only acts on Read.
Subagent exemption — agent_id present means a subagent is invoking, exempt from line limits.

pre_existing_guards.code_snippet: _, ext = os.path.splitext(file_path); return ext.lower() in BINARY_EXTENSIONS
return isinstance(requested_limit, int) and 0 < requested_limit <= CHUNK_LIMIT
pre_existing_guards.purpose: Bounded chunk carve-out — allow offset+limit Reads when chunk size <= 600.
Only act when tool is EnterWorktree.

items.evidence: Not a regression — bypass gap present from hook inception. Git log of both files confirms threshold-only tweaks (150efdb, 9204c25); no commit added then removed the /do/allow bypass branches.

findings: N/A — hook chain itself IS the middleware; full chain audited via Codex audit (8214 lines) and direct Read of suspect files

items.check: build / deploy / dependency changes ruled out
items.evidence: Settings.json registers both hooks unchanged. No build step involved — hooks are interpreted scripts.

setup.reason: non-UI -- hook + config change; cycle does not produce (1) rendered UI changes, (2) browser interaction, (3) Playwright invocation, (4) screenshot evidence, or (5) any change to user-triggered code paths (pipeline steps, API endpoints). Only PreToolUse hook scripts are touched; they fire on every main-agent tool call and emit only stderr + exit codes.

evidence.observed: User said '/do 还是出问题' — they had to delegate to a subagent because /do failed to unlock Read on a large file. Codex audit confirmed by direct hook invocation: with /do flag set AND /allow Read granted, pretool-read-size-guard.py still returned exit 2 on a 1001-line file.

measured.value: pretool-read-size-guard.py:21-25 contains agent_id subagent-exemption but no /do consent flag check and no /allow read_grant call. The hook imports json/os/sys only — no import of lib.allowlist, no Path import, no /tmp/claude-orchestrator-consent flag reference anywhere in the file (lines 1-75).

expected.value: Main-agent Read should pass when /tmp/claude-orchestrator-consent-<sid>.flag exists with content exactly 'true', OR when lib.allowlist.read_grant('Read', sid) returns True. Canonical idiom from pretool-orchestrator-gate.py:66-71 (has_consent) + :177-185 (read_grant short-circuit). Parallel fix for EnterWorktree per CLAUDE.md:49 carve-out.
expected.source: /root/.claude/CLAUDE.md:48-50 (documented /do contract); /root/.claude/hooks/pretool-orchestrator-gate.py:66-71 + :177-185 (canonical implementation); /root/.claude/commands/do.md:1-4 (description: 'Allow main agent to bypass orchestrator-gate restrictions for this turn (subagent-only operations become directly allowed)')

evidence.gap: Two PreToolUse hooks (pretool-read-size-guard.py, pretool-block-enterworktree.sh) are missing the /do consent flag check and /allow read_grant call. Additionally, pretool-read-size-guard.py has LINE_LIMIT=1000 while docstring and CLAUDE.md both say 600 — a 400-line drift introduced by commit 9204c25 (May 7) inside a daily-trade defects batch.

reference_source.description: Canonical /do + /allow bypass idioms from sibling hooks in the same repo, all read end-to-end in this session.

attempts.proposed_solution: Raise LINE_LIMIT 200->600 because 'Main agent's direct Read limit was too tight for legitimate files'
attempts.actual_change: LINE_LIMIT constant changed; block message refined to steer agent toward Grep-narrow-read or subagent summarization
attempts.outcome: Partially helped — fewer files tripped the limit. /do bypass coverage still completely missing; user still hit the wall on files >600 lines.

attempts.proposed_solution: LOW-9 in daily-trade defects batch: 'read-size guard cap 1000 + chunked carve-out'
attempts.actual_change: LINE_LIMIT raised 600->1000; added CHUNK_LIMIT=600 + is_bounded_chunk() carve-out for offset+limit Reads
attempts.outcome: Helped users who could chunk their reads; /do bypass coverage still missing; user with full-file Read >1000 lines still blocked. Created docstring/CLAUDE.md drift (both still say 600).

novelty_check.rationale: Both prior attempts were L3 (threshold/constant tweaks). This attempt adds L4 logic branches (consent flag check, grant read) that actually wire up the documented /do/allow contract. It also reverts the L3 drift introduced by attempt 2 to align code with docstring + CLAUDE.md.

context.codebase_state: Clean working tree on master at ba64db2. Two hook files unmodified since 9204c25 (May 7) and a much earlier checkpoint for block-enterworktree.sh.
context.recent_commits: ba64db2 (orphan changes), c411ef1 (gitignore), a03025c (qa artifacts), e78ce03 (auto-bulk commit), ea02581 (auto-bulk commit). For affected files: 150efdb raised LINE_LIMIT 200->600 (Apr 16); 9204c25 raised LINE_LIMIT 600->1000 and added CHUNK_LIMIT (May 7).

environment.hook_protocol: PreToolUse stdin JSON per Claude Code spec

development_approach.strategy: Two surgical patches, each adopting the canonical bypass idiom from an already-honoring sibling hook. PATCH-1 (Python) follows pretool-orchestrator-gate.py:66-71 + :177-185. PATCH-2 (Bash) follows pretool-write-guard.sh:17-46. PATCH-1 additionally restores LINE_LIMIT=600 to reconcile code with docstring and CLAUDE.md. No new dependencies, no refactors, no changes to existing function bodies — only new pass-through branches inserted at well-defined positions.
development_approach.validation_approach: QA invokes each modified hook directly with crafted stdin JSON payloads, simulating: (1) main agent + /do flag set, (2) main agent + /allow grant set, (3) subagent (agent_id present), (4) plain main agent. Verifies exit codes (0 for AC1-3, AC6-7; 2 for AC4, AC8) and stderr content (AC8 message verbatim). Verifies LINE_LIMIT value via grep (AC5). Verifies no diff in the 9 already-honoring hooks via git diff (AC9).

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex confirmed all four findings, refined the bypass-coverage rationale, and surfaced two adjacent observations that are correctly out-of-scope. Specifically: (1) PATCH-1 bypass branch MUST gate on tool_name=='Read' AND not subagent (the hook is globally registered, so unconditional read_grant calls would fire on every tool); (2) /do flag check MUST use content == 'true', not bare flag.exists() — codex pointed at orchestrator-gate.py:66-71 as the canonical idiom; (3) FINDING-4 EnterWorktree is in-scope per documented contract but framing must say 'documented /do carve-out coverage', NOT 'subagent parity' (the hook currently blocks subagents too — no parity gap exists); (4) PATCH-2 stdin-JSON switch is safer than CLAUDE_TOOL_NAME env, fail-open on parse, derive sid from JSON then env then 'default'. Two observations confirmed correct out-of-scope decisions on workflow-gate and the read_grant substring/exact asymmetry.

findings.proposed_fix: Add main-agent-only bypass for tool_name=='Read' after JSON parse and before line-count enforcement
findings.rationale: Matches my draft plan exactly; encoded in Technical Hints PATCH-1 sketch with explicit gate on tool_name=='Read' AND not agent_id.

findings.proposed_fix: Restore LINE_LIMIT=600 in same patch as bypass branches; cohesive doc-vs-code reconciliation
findings.rationale: Encoded as M3 with rationale that the user-facing contract is the CLAUDE.md whitelist, which says 600.

findings.issue: PATCH-1 subtlety: do not call read_grant unconditionally for every tool
findings.proposed_fix: Gate the bypass branch on tool_name=='Read' AND not subagent AND (consent flag or read_grant)
findings.rationale: Encoded in PATCH-1 sketch: the new branch sits AFTER tool_name=='Read' check (via is_exempt's existing early-exit) but BEFORE the line-count enforcement, and explicitly gates on `data.get('tool_name') == 'Read' and not data.get('agent_id')`. Hook is globally registered so this matters.

findings.issue: Use flag.exists() AND content == 'true', not exists-only
findings.proposed_fix: Mirror pretool-orchestrator-gate.py:66-71 exactly: flag.exists() and flag.read_text().strip() == 'true'
findings.rationale: Encoded verbatim in PATCH-1 sketch and explicitly cited as Risk 2 mitigation.

findings.issue: FINDING-4 framing: not subagent parity, just documented carve-out
findings.proposed_fix: Adjust rationale in spec to say 'documented /do contract' not 'subagent parity'; pretool-block-enterworktree.sh currently blocks subagents too
findings.rationale: Encoded as Risk 4 in spec and as 'Important nuance' in FINDING-4 evidence block. Out-of-scope observations explicitly state subagents stay subject to EnterWorktree block.

findings.issue: PATCH-2 stdin JSON switch is correct
findings.proposed_fix: Switch from CLAUDE_TOOL_NAME env to stdin JSON; fail-open on parse; derive sid from JSON then env then 'default'; main-agent-only bypass
findings.rationale: Encoded in PATCH-2 sketch following pretool-write-guard.sh:17-46 idiom exactly. Risk 3 documents the regression risk and the mitigation.

findings.issue: Test ACs should use exact tool-name allow patterns
findings.proposed_fix: AC2/AC7 should specify pattern == 'Read' / 'EnterWorktree' exactly to avoid exposing the read_grant substring asymmetry
findings.rationale: AC2 and AC7 now specify exact-match grants. The substring asymmetry is logged in out_of_scope_observations as an adjacent issue.

findings.issue: Workflow-gate left untouched is OK if explicitly justified
findings.rationale: FINDING-2 out-of-scope rationale documented in spec Out-of-Scope Observations and in the context JSON out_of_scope_observations[].

findings.issue: read_grant PreToolUse substring vs PostToolUse exact asymmetry
findings.rationale: Adjacent issue — not in user-need path. Logged in out_of_scope_observations[] with explicit security_relevant=false. Tests use exact patterns to avoid exposing the asymmetry.

findings.issue: If '全修' means '/allow bypasses every blocking hook', ticket is incomplete
findings.rationale: User-need path is the /do/allow bypass contract documented in CLAUDE.md, which is about orchestrator-gate tool-class tiering. The other safety hooks (bash-views-guard, claude-config-guard, etc.) are all-agent integrity hooks, not subagent-class permission gates. Logged in out_of_scope_observations[] with explicit framing.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: All four blocker/major findings applied to PATCH-1 and PATCH-2 sketches and to the spec's evidence/risks/rationale sections. Three observation_only findings logged in out_of_scope_observations[]. No silent scope expansion; all out-of-scope decisions explicitly enumerated.

research_findings.reason: Bug fix with well-defined root cause; canonical fix idiom exists in 8 sibling hooks in the same repo. Web research would not add value over the in-session evidence.
